transcript.clean_text: stage five of the toronto adriatico bright yet cold the action however extremely hot on a short 155 kilometer run to fermo well it was characterized by some hellish climbs many of which not categorized and not least the first of the day which drew out an amazing response with many riders battling for a place in any prospective breakaway a downhillish run to the foot of a non-categorized test that really saw several incarnations of great breakaways and in the end we finally had 12 up the road by a handsome margin maximum gap was over four minutes and they were being pegged back by a committed uae team emirates team well emirates started to lose the will to do all of the driving uh the main problem was that in the breakaway was benjamin thomas of coffee this starting the day a minute and eight in arrears of telepocature overall and potentially the man that was going to deny the breakaway their fun well as the king of the mountains points started to get handed out francesco gabazzi took some for aylo cometa and we were heading for yet more climbing even with the intermediate sprint but really the distillation of the day was a series of tests on a circuit that would take us up to firma some of the ramps were absolutely punishing up to 21 and the man who was equal to it from the breakaway warren barghee clemen rousseau his teammate had done so much work that barghi offered up some of the champagne at the end of the day and by that you can probably realize that barghi had the medal to finish the job five years around since his last big win and this was going to be a huge one as the breakaway fell away benjamin thomas regrouped with the man up front there were others that still remain hopeful such as sandra maurice nelson oliveira who were also a part of that original breakaway but really it was down surely to what warren burghy could do they were leaning on him would he take a stand well he was timing it absolutely brilliantly meanwhile timing was the thing in the chase ramco and nepal decided that now was the time to go had ue team emirates used up too many of their battlers protecting telepocatcha well pagachi in the blue jersey following straight on to the white jersey he's loaning to evan nepal at the moment and they were taking some great corners out there unfortunately one of them was missed vinegar was also in the mix there gianluca brambia who dropped back out of the breakaway suddenly found himself in a happier place well it wasn't a happy place at all for remco evan nepal of the three that missed the turn he was the worst off turning quickly was pogacchia he getting back in but the struggle for emma paul what could have been a great day for him taking an advance perhaps on talibacher but pagatcha looked strong of those up front warren barghee was absolutely the strongest and there was no chasing him down when he set off finally on the last series of climbs he determined to hit the cobbles first and that's precisely what he did and as the road picked up so did his pace he was absolutely majestic the gap back to the blue jersey 51 seconds it wasn't going to be the day of benjamin thomas but another frenchman warren barghe would bring it home well he doesn't win often but when he does win he wins big grand tour stages tour de france welter have gone his way this will be a huge phillip to a man who is not done yet at the age of 30. taking the final bend this must have been extra sweet because of the distance between his last victory and this one magnificent performance and proving that unpredictability can creep into the day it's not all about tally pikachu though he still leads this race sandra maurice coming home in second place ahead of simoni velasco of astana and the roster of riders who came to congratulate warren barghe was long burgetchip coming home same time as remco ever nepal and the owners being a go bodes well for a good battle in the mountains today's battler though warren buggy margin of victory 10 seconds over maurice and velasco coming home behind oliveira port bogacher vingo the smiles of barghi we were used to seeing it some years ago well it's back could it be the start of something special we should wait and see pogaccha leads evan appall by nine seconds ironsman on 43 vinca go 45 lopez rounding out the top five a minute adrift same time as richie port who was lively today so what of stage six an earlier start for everybody and two mighty tests at the end of a 216 kilometer run in total to carpenter a nasty stage certainly and those two big peaks at the very end with the downhill run kicking up towards the line but no mountain top finish essentially tomorrow but two big peaks to take on surely bulgacha is the favorite we shall see
